records.ety: [OF. cote, F. cotte, petticoat, cotte d'armes coat of arms, cotte de mailles coat of mail, LL. cota, cotta, tunic, prob. of German origin; cf. OHG. chozzo coarse mantle, G. klotze, D. kot, hut, E. cot. Cf. Cot a hut.]

defs: An outer garment fitting the upper part of the body; especially, such a garment worn by men.
quotes: Let each His adamantine coat gird well.

defs: The habit or vesture of an order of men, indicating the order or office; cloth.
quotes: Men of his coat should be minding their prayers.
She was sought by spirits of richest coat.

defs: An external covering like a garment, as fur, skin, wool, husk, or bark; as, the horses coats were sleek.
quotes: Fruit of all kinds, in coat Rough or smooth rined, or bearded husk, or shell.

defs: A layer of any substance covering another; a cover; a tegument; as, the coats of the eye; the coats of an onion; a coat of tar or varnish.
Same as Coat of arms. See below.
quotes: Hark, countrymen! either renew the fight, Or tear the lions out of England's coat.
Here's a trick of discarded cards of us! We were ranked with coats as long as old master lived.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Coated; p. pr. & vb. n. Coating.]

defs: To cover with a coat or outer garment.
To cover with a layer of any substance; as, to coat a jar with tin foil; to coat a ceiling.
